_comment: Unique tile definitions for Realm of Shadow. These are special one-of-a-kind map features that enrich the world with lore, landmarks, secrets, and interactive points of interest. Each tile can have a visible sprite or be invisible (discovered only by stepping on it). Used across overworld, dungeon, and town maps.

moongate.name: Moongate
moongate.description: A shimmering ring of blue light hovers above the ground. The air crackles with energy. Where it leads, none can say.
moongate.interact_text: You step through the moongate. The world blurs and reforms around you.

whispering_stones.name: Whispering Stones
whispering_stones.description: Three tall standing stones arranged in a triangle. When the wind blows through them, you can almost hear words.
whispering_stones.interact_text: The stones whisper: 'Seek the shadow beneath the mountain. The key lies where light cannot reach.'

_categories._comment: Reference for tile categories used above.
_categories.landmark: Visible points of interest — shrines, ruins, notable features
_categories.portal: Teleportation points — moongates, tunnels, magical doorways
_categories.secret: Hidden discoveries — buried caches, hidden rooms, invisible features
_categories.lore: World-building text — memorials, inscriptions, environmental storytelling
_categories.rest: Safe havens for healing and recovery
_categories.dungeon_feature: Special tiles found only in dungeons

_interact_types._comment: Reference for interaction types used above.
_interact_types.loot: Give items or gold to the party
_interact_types.trap: One-time damage with a saving throw
_interact_types.teleport: Move party to a new location
_interact_types.wish: Random beneficial effect for a gold cost
_interact_types.rest: Heal HP and MP, safe from encounters
_interact_types.obstacle: Blocked passage requiring specific items or abilities
